name: Accompanying documents
pageTitle: Documents - Import and export applications - GOV.UK
caption: Documents
heading: Accompanying documents
bodyCopy: Upload any required documents, for example:
if applicable, a health or phytosanitary certificate (also known as the ‘official certificate’) and laboratory sample results
transport documents, such as a road consignment (CMR) note or air, sea or rail waybills
commercial documents, such as bill of lading, invoice and packing list
layout: govuk-grid-row > govuk-grid-column-two-thirds (with a nested govuk-grid-column-full for the documents grid)

structure.text: Upload any required documents, for example:

if applicable, a health or phytosanitary certificate (also known as the ‘official certificate’) and laboratory sample results / transport documents, such as a road consignment (CMR) note or air, sea or rail waybills / commercial documents, such as bill of lading, invoice and packing list

structure.govukComponent: Heading (govuk-heading-s) — dynamic count of added rows

structure.text: Date of issue / Day Month Year
Document issue date (Day / Month / Year) with a 'Choose date' calendar reveal
View {filename} — an uploaded attachment renders in the row's 'Attachments' column as a 'View <filename>' link (e.g. 'View example.png')
structure.evidence: page-objects/notification/AccompanyingDocumentsPage.ts:31-33 (locatorAttachmentLink returns getByRole('link', { name: `View ${filename}` })); asserted visible after a valid CHED-D upload in tests/document/document-upload-valid-commercial-invoice.spec.ts:38

structure.text: Success notification region shown after a valid upload (contains '…document uploaded')

LEGACY: the success banner is rendered by the {{#if banner.display}} govuk/notificationBanner (classes govuk-notification-banner--success) at ipaffs-frontend-notification/service/src/views/importer/documents.html:13-21.

Bulleted list of example documents to upload

trace b3742f19b6eea5d9285f5c8739571523a0a13ec1 action 36; page object getByLabel('Document type') at page-objects/notification/AccompanyingDocumentsPage.ts:7-9; LEGACY: govuk/select topOption='Select document type' ariaLabel='Document type' at ipaffs-frontend-notification/service/src/views/partials/common/documents/inputAdditionalDocumentRow.html:6-15

nonStandardPatterns.pattern: additional-documents-table, additional-documents__grid-row, additional-documents__grid-column-15, additional-documents__grid-column-35, additional-documents__grid-column-doc-date, additional-documents__table-header, additional-documents__headers
nonStandardPatterns.whatItDoes: A bespoke CSS-grid 'table' laying out the four column headers (Document type / Document reference / Date of issue / Attachments) and each added document row across the full width. Not a real <table>; div-based grid with IPAFFS-specific class names.
nonStandardPatterns.concern: This is a hand-rolled multi-column data-entry grid — not the GOV.UK Table component and not a documented GOV.UK pattern. It couples layout to custom CSS and screen-reader semantics rely on ad-hoc markup rather than table roles.
nonStandardPatterns.govukAlternative: For repeating add-more rows, the GOV.UK 'Add another' pattern (component in govuk-frontend / the add-another Nunjucks component) plus a Summary list or Table to display added rows would replace this. The new app should render each added document as a Summary list card and use a standard Table/Summary list for review.
nonStandardPatterns.evidence: trace eddc6f6ec852d692c454d319ade1607727a84a4f action 33 (class inventory); LEGACY: div-based grid markup at ipaffs-frontend-notification/service/src/views/importer/documents.html:312-330 and partials/common/documents/inputAdditionalDocumentRow.html:1-48

nonStandardPatterns.pattern: defra-datepicker, date-input--picker, date-picker__container, date-picker__container--icon, date-picker__reveal__icon, date-picker__button__previous-month, date-picker__button__next-month, date-picker__button__close, date-picker__dialog, date-picker__dialog--hidden, date-picker__date-table, date-picker__heading, date-picker__header, date-picker-day, date-picker-month, date-picker-year, date-input-day, date-input-month, date-input-year, short-date-input
nonStandardPatterns.whatItDoes: A custom DEFRA calendar date-picker widget layered on top of the govuk-date-input. Adds a 'Choose date' icon button that opens a month-grid calendar dialog (July 2026 shown, Previous/Next month, Su Mo Tu We Th Fr Sa table, Cancel) to pick the document issue date.
nonStandardPatterns.concern: The GOV.UK Design System deliberately does NOT ship a calendar date picker — it recommends the three-field Day/Month/Year Date input for known dates. This bespoke calendar is extra JS, extra a11y surface (dialog, aria-live) and outside the toolbox.
nonStandardPatterns.govukAlternative: GOV.UK Date input (govuk-date-input) alone — which is already present underneath. The new app should drop the calendar overlay and keep only the Day/Month/Year fields.
nonStandardPatterns.evidence: trace eddc6f6ec852d692c454d319ade1607727a84a4f action 33 (class inventory + structure: 'Choose date', 'July 2026', Previous/Next month buttons); LEGACY: classes='short-date-input' applied to govuk/date at inputAdditionalDocumentRow.html:34 triggers the datepicker enhancement

nonStandardPatterns.whatItDoes: <button> elements styled to look like links, used for 'Add attachment' and 'Add a document'.
nonStandardPatterns.concern: Custom class for a button-as-link; not a standard govuk-button (nor a plain link). The GOV.UK approach is either a secondary button or a genuine link.
nonStandardPatterns.govukAlternative: govuk-button--secondary for the add actions, or the Add another component's built-in add/remove buttons.
nonStandardPatterns.evidence: trace eddc6f6ec852d692c454d319ade1607727a84a4f action 33 (structure: BUTTON.link-button 'Add attachment', BUTTON.link-button 'Add a document'); LEGACY: <button class="link-button ..."> for 'Add attachment' at inputAdditionalDocumentRow.html:41-46 and for 'Add a document' at documents.html:351-366

nonStandardPatterns.whatItDoes: A visually-hidden aria-live region used to announce datepicker / row changes to assistive tech.
nonStandardPatterns.concern: Bespoke live-region plumbing tied to the custom widgets above; would disappear if the custom datepicker/grid were replaced by standard components. 'sr-only' duplicates govuk-visually-hidden.
nonStandardPatterns.govukAlternative: govuk-visually-hidden for hidden text; standard components manage their own announcements.

nonStandardPatterns.pattern: govuk-caption-xl / govuk-heading-s used as pseudo table-header captions inside a custom grid; govuk-clearfix
nonStandardPatterns.whatItDoes: govuk-* utility classes used to style the bespoke grid; govuk-clearfix floats the custom columns.
nonStandardPatterns.concern: Reliance on float clearing (govuk-clearfix) signals a float-based bespoke layout rather than fl-grid/summary-list; symptomatic of the custom table.
nonStandardPatterns.govukAlternative: Replaced implicitly once the custom grid becomes an Add another + Summary list.

fields.name: document-type
fields.label: Document type
fields.requiredEvidence: LEGACY (row-conditional) — for CHED-D (not isChedp), once a document row is started the document-type field is required: Joi.string().valid(...documentTypeMap keys).required() with all three messages (ANY_VALID/ANY_REQUIRED/STRING_EMPTY) set to DOCUMENT_TYPE at ipaffs-frontend-notification/service/src/validation/routes/handlers/importer/documents_validation.js:46-52. 'Started' = any of type/reference/date filled OR Add-a-document/Add-attachment clicked (shouldValidateAdditionalDocument, documents_validation.js:30-33). NOTE: this is a PAGE/frontend requiredness policy, not a model @NotNull — the schema AccompanyingDocument.documentType has NO CED-group @NotNull (defaults to null; ipaffs-imports-notification-schema/.../representation/AccompanyingDocument.java:32-33). The rebuild may revisit: adding a document at all is optional, but IF a row is begun all three fields become mandatory.
fields.conditionalOn: Document row is shown (a row is present by default; more via 'Add a document')
fields.evidence: trace b3742f19b6eea5d9285f5c8739571523a0a13ec1 action 36; option values from trace eddc6f6ec852d692c454d319ade1607727a84a4f action 33 select eval (value=text pairs: =Select document type, healthCertificate, airWaybill, billOfLading, originCertificate, commercialInvoice, listOfIngredients, laboratorySamplingResultsForAflatoxin, packingList, phytosanitaryCertificate, railwayBill, roadConsignmentNote, seaWaybill, other). CORROBORATION: driven via getByLabel('Document type') at page-objects/notification/AccompanyingDocumentsPage.ts:7-9; the tests only ever select 'Health certificate' (documentType.healthCertificate — the de-facto default, workflows/notification/ched-d-workflows.ts:93,240 and :627). The full domain vocabulary types/document-type.ts (27 entries, a cross-CHED superset) is a strict superset of the 14 CHED-D-rendered options — do NOT treat the domain list as the CHED-D option set; the 14 confirmed by the trace stand. LEGACY: the option set is data-driven from documentTypeMap(notification) (documents_validation.js:14,47) — the .valid(...) list is exactly the keys of that map, so the 14 rendered options are the authoritative CHED-D allow-list. NOTE the visible 'Laboratory sampling results' maps to value=laboratorySamplingResultsForAflatoxin in the CHED-D trace, whereas the domain map (document-type.ts:15) labels that same value 'Laboratory Sampling results for Aflatoxin (Reg 2019/1793)' — CHED-D re-labels it; recorded in openQuestions.

fields.name: document-reference
fields.label: Document reference
fields.requiredEvidence: LEGACY (row-conditional) — for CHED-D (not isChedp) the reference is Joi.string().required().max(100) once a row is started: STRING_EMPTY/ANY_REQUIRED -> requiredMessage(REFERENCE, A) = 'Enter a reference'; STRING_MAX -> maxLengthMessage(DOCUMENT_REFERENCE, 100) = 'Document reference must be 100 characters or fewer'. getReferenceValidation(isChedp) at documents_validation.js:53,123-130 (isChedp gets .optional().allow(''); CHED-D does NOT). Model-level: AccompanyingDocument.documentReference has no CED @NotNull (AccompanyingDocument.java:34-35) — page-level policy the rebuild may revisit.
fields.evidence: trace eddc6f6ec852d692c454d319ade1607727a84a4f action 33 (fill 'REF-124', id=document-reference, name=document-reference). CORROBORATION: driven via getByRole('textbox', { name: 'Document reference' }) at page-objects/notification/AccompanyingDocumentsPage.ts:11-13; default value 'REF-124' (workflows/notification/ched-d-workflows.ts:94,241). Value persists and displays downstream as 'REF-124' on the inspector documents view (tests/document/document-access.spec.ts:63). LEGACY: 100-char max is now known from getReferenceValidation (documents_validation.js:125).

fields.name: document-issue-date-day / document-issue-date-month / document-issue-date-year
fields.label: Date of issue
fields.requiredEvidence: LEGACY (row-conditional) — for CHED-D the issue date is required once a row is started: dateValidator.dateValidation({ ..., label: DOCUMENT_DATE_LABEL='Date of issue', path: DOCUMENT_DATE_KEY, required: !isChedp }) — required=true for CHED-D (documents_validation.js:97-108; DOCUMENT_DATE_LABEL at utils/validation_constants.js:156). No past/future constraint is applied (no checkIsInPast/checkIsInFuture), consistent with the QA copy-as-new using a -30-day relative date being accepted. Model-level: AccompanyingDocument.documentIssueDate (LocalDate) has no CED @NotNull (AccompanyingDocument.java:37-40) — page-level policy the rebuild may revisit.
fields.conditionalOn: Document row is shown; fieldset legend 'Document issue date' is visually hidden, visible column header 'Date of issue' + 'Day Month Year' hint
fields.evidence: trace b3742f19b6eea5d9285f5c8739571523a0a13ec1 actions 38/39/40 (fill Day '04', Month '03', Year '2023'); controls from trace eddc6f6ec852d692c454d319ade1607727a84a4f action 33 (names document-issue-date-day/-month/-year). A custom 'Choose date' calendar picker also sets these. CORROBORATION: driven via getByRole('textbox', { name: 'Day' / 'Month' / 'Year' }) at page-objects/notification/AccompanyingDocumentsPage.ts:15-51 (fillDocumentDate); default 04/03/2023 (workflows/notification/ched-d-workflows.ts:95-99,242). Copy-as-new uses a relative date getRelativeDateTime(-30) (ched-d-workflows.ts:629) — an in-the-past date is accepted. LEGACY: govuk/date name prefix 'document-issue-date', legend 'Document issue date' visually hidden at inputAdditionalDocumentRow.html:28-38; day range 1-31, month range 1-12, year > 1969 (utils/validation_constants.js:327-331).

fields.name: fileUpload
fields.label: Select a file
fields.hint: Your file must be: smaller than 10MB; larger than 1KB; file type must be a DOC, JPEG, PDF, PNG or XLS; ZIP files are not allowed for security reasons
fields.requiredEvidence: LEGACY — adding an attachment is optional. The file itself is only validated on the upload sub-step; the row's Save does not force an attachment.
fields.conditionalOn: Shown after clicking 'Add attachment' — a 'Select a file' upload sub-step (page title becomes 'Accompanying documents - Import and export applications - GOV.UK') with Next and Cancel buttons
fields.evidence: trace b3742f19b6eea5d9285f5c8739571523a0a13ec1 action 42 (input[type=file] name=fileUpload, class govuk-file-upload; main innerText lists the file constraints and Next/Cancel). CORROBORATION: page-objects/notification/DocumentUploadPage.ts:22-24 (inputFile = locator('input[type="file"]')); the sub-step's primary button is labelled either 'Next' OR 'Continue' (regex /^(Next|Continue)$/ at DocumentUploadPage.ts:14-16). Test confirms JPG, PNG, DOC, DOCX, XLS, PDF all upload successfully (tests/document/document-upload-valid-commercial-invoice.spec.ts:8, CHED-D describe :28-41). LEGACY (resolves the DOCX question): the authoritative allow-list is ALLOWED_EXTENSION = {csv, doc, docx, jpg, jpeg, pdf, png, xlsx, xls, gif} at ipaffs-file-upload-microservice/service/src/main/java/uk/gov/defra/tracesx/file/upload/service/ValidationService.java:26-27 — so DOCX, CSV, XLSX and GIF are ALL accepted even though the on-page hint (confirmed) advertises only 'DOC, JPEG, PDF, PNG or XLS'. Size limits: reject > 10MB (TEN_MB) and reject < 200 bytes (TWO_HUNDRED_B) — note the code threshold is 200 bytes, not the 1KB the message/hint claim (ValidationService.java:24-28,49-51). On valid upload the attachment renders in the row as a 'View <filename>' link (AccompanyingDocumentsPage.ts:31-33) and a Success banner appears.

validationMessages.message: Select a document type
validationMessages.trigger: CHED-D: a document row is started (any of type/reference/date filled, or 'Add a document'/'Add attachment' clicked) but no document type is selected
validationMessages.evidence: ipaffs-frontend-notification/service/src/validation/routes/handlers/importer/documents_validation.js:46-52 (Joi .valid(...).required() with ANY_VALID/ANY_REQUIRED/STRING_EMPTY all mapped to DOCUMENT_TYPE) -> DOCUMENT_TYPE = 'Select a document type' at src/validation/messages/en.js:71. Row-conditional trigger via shouldValidateAdditionalDocument (documents_validation.js:30-33). Not observed in either captured trace.

validationMessages.message: Enter a reference
validationMessages.trigger: CHED-D: a document row is started but Document reference is empty
validationMessages.evidence: ipaffs-frontend-notification/service/src/validation/routes/handlers/importer/documents_validation.js:53,123-130 (getReferenceValidation: non-CHED-P is Joi.string().required(); STRING_EMPTY/ANY_REQUIRED -> requiredMessage(REFERENCE, A)). requiredMessage = `Enter ${article} ${label.toLowerCase()}` (src/validation/messages/en.js:31-33), REFERENCE = 'Reference' (src/validation/labels/en.js:45), A = 'a' (labels/en.js:2) => 'Enter a reference'. Not observed in either captured trace.

validationMessages.message: Document reference must be 100 characters or fewer
validationMessages.trigger: Document reference exceeds 100 characters
validationMessages.evidence: documents_validation.js:125-128 (.max(100), STRING_MAX -> maxLengthMessage(DOCUMENT_REFERENCE, 100)). maxLengthMessage = `${label} must be ${maxLength} characters or fewer` (messages/en.js:3-5), DOCUMENT_REFERENCE = 'Document reference' (labels/en.js:44). Not observed in either captured trace.

validationMessages.message: Enter a date of issue
validationMessages.trigger: CHED-D: a document row is started but the issue date is entirely empty (no day, month or year)
validationMessages.evidence: documents_validation.js:97-108 (dateValidation, required=!isChedp=true, label DOCUMENT_DATE_LABEL='Date of issue') -> validateRequiredFields with DATE_MISSING_ERROR(label) = `Enter a ${label.toLowerCase()}` (date_validator.js:99-106; validation_messages.js:13). DOCUMENT_DATE_LABEL at utils/validation_constants.js:156. Not observed in either captured trace.

validationMessages.message: Date of issue must include a day
validationMessages.trigger: Issue date has month and year but no day
validationMessages.evidence: date_validator.js:108-127 (validateIncompleteness -> DAY_MISSING_ERROR(label)); validation_messages.js:14 = `${label} must include a day`, label 'Date of issue'. Not observed in either captured trace.

validationMessages.message: Date of issue must include a month
validationMessages.trigger: Issue date has day and year but no month
validationMessages.evidence: date_validator.js:108-127 (MONTH_MISSING_ERROR); validation_messages.js:15. Not observed in either captured trace.

validationMessages.message: Date of issue must include a year
validationMessages.trigger: Issue date has day and month but no year
validationMessages.evidence: date_validator.js:108-127 (YEAR_MISSING_ERROR); validation_messages.js:16. Not observed in either captured trace.

validationMessages.message: Date of issue must include a day and month
validationMessages.trigger: Issue date has year only
validationMessages.evidence: date_validator.js:108-127 (DAY_AND_MONTH_MISSING_ERROR); validation_messages.js:17. Not observed in either captured trace.

validationMessages.message: Date of issue must include a month and year
validationMessages.trigger: Issue date has day only
validationMessages.evidence: date_validator.js:108-127 (MONTH_AND_YEAR_MISSING_ERROR); validation_messages.js:18. Not observed in either captured trace.

validationMessages.message: Date of issue must include a day and year
validationMessages.trigger: Issue date has month only
validationMessages.evidence: date_validator.js:108-127 (DAY_AND_YEAR_MISSING_ERROR); validation_messages.js:19. Not observed in either captured trace.

validationMessages.message: Day must be from 1 to 31
validationMessages.trigger: Day is not a number or outside 1-31
validationMessages.evidence: date_validator.js:129-157 (validateDateRange, RANGE_VALIDATION_ERROR('Day', MIN_DAY, MAX_DAY)); RANGE_VALIDATION_ERROR = `${label} must be from ${minValue} to ${maxValue}` (validation_messages.js:20); MIN_DAY=1, MAX_DAY=31 (utils/validation_constants.js:327-328). Error attaches to path document-issue-date-day. Not observed in either captured trace.

validationMessages.message: Month must be from 1 to 12
validationMessages.trigger: Month is not a number or outside 1-12
validationMessages.evidence: date_validator.js:129-157 (RANGE_VALIDATION_ERROR('Month', MIN_MONTH, MAX_MONTH)); MIN_MONTH=1, MAX_MONTH=12 (utils/validation_constants.js:329-330). Not observed in either captured trace.

validationMessages.message: Year must be 4 numbers
validationMessages.trigger: Year is not exactly 4 digits
validationMessages.evidence: date_validator.js:159-172 (validateYear -> MAX_YEAR_LENGTH_VALIDATION_ERROR('Year')); validation_messages.js:21 = `${label} must be 4 numbers`. Not observed in either captured trace.

validationMessages.message: Year must be later than 1969
validationMessages.trigger: Year is 4 digits but <= 1969
validationMessages.evidence: date_validator.js:159-172 (validateYear -> MIN_YEAR_VALIDATION_ERROR('Year', MIN_YEAR)); validation_messages.js:22 = `${label} must be later than ${minYear}`; MIN_YEAR=1969 (utils/validation_constants.js:331). Not observed in either captured trace.

validationMessages.message: Date of issue must be a real date
validationMessages.trigger: Day/month/year individually valid but the combination is not a real calendar date (e.g. 31/02)
validationMessages.evidence: date_validator.js:174-181 (validateValidDate -> INVALID_DATE_ERROR(label)); validation_messages.js:23 = `${label} must be a real date`. Not observed in either captured trace.

validationMessages.message: The selected file must be a DOC, JPEG, PDF, PNG, XLS or GIF
validationMessages.trigger: Attachment file extension is not in the allow-list {csv, doc, docx, jpg, jpeg, pdf, png, xlsx, xls, gif}
validationMessages.evidence: ipaffs-file-upload-microservice/service/src/main/java/uk/gov/defra/tracesx/file/upload/service/ValidationService.java:16-17,26-27,37-41 (FILE_TYPE_IS_INCORRECT thrown when extension not in ALLOWED_EXTENSION); same constant in MultiUploadValidationService.java:18. NOTE this legacy copy NAMES GIF, whereas the confirmed on-page hint and the test-inferred reject message below both omit it — see discrepancies. Not observed in either captured trace.

validationMessages.message: The selected file must be a DOC, JPEG, PDF, PNG or XLS
validationMessages.trigger: Attachment file is a disallowed type (e.g. ZIP or TXT)
validationMessages.evidence: tests/document/document-upload-invalid-commercial-invoice.spec.ts:9-10 (message) + CHED-D describe block :33-46 (createChedDNotification({stopAtAccompanyingDocuments:true}) -> Add attachment -> upload example.zip / example.txt -> expect error summary toContainText). Rendered on the 'Select a file' upload sub-step error summary (role=alert, DocumentUploadPage.ts:26-28). CONFLICTS with the legacy FILE_TYPE_IS_INCORRECT copy above ('...XLS or GIF') — the QA test asserts a shorter string via toContainText, so it does not disprove GIF; see discrepancies. NOT observed in either captured trace.

validationMessages.message: The selected file is empty
validationMessages.trigger: Attachment file is 0 bytes
validationMessages.evidence: ipaffs-file-upload-microservice/.../service/ValidationService.java:19,45-47 (FILE_IS_EMPTY thrown when fileSize == 0); same constant MultiUploadValidationService.java:19. CORROBORATION: tests/document/document-upload-invalid-commercial-invoice.spec.ts:11 (upload empty-file.pdf). Not observed in either captured trace.

validationMessages.message: The selected file must be larger than 1KB
validationMessages.trigger: Attachment file is non-empty but under the minimum size (code threshold 200 bytes)
validationMessages.evidence: ipaffs-file-upload-microservice/.../service/ValidationService.java:18,24-25,49-51 (FILE_TOO_SMALL thrown when fileSize < TWO_HUNDRED_B=200); same constant MultiUploadValidationService.java:22 (FILE_SMALLER_THAN_1KB). NOTE the message says '1KB' but the enforced threshold is 200 bytes — see discrepancies. New message not previously in the spec. Not observed in either captured trace.

validationMessages.message: The selected file must be smaller than 10MB
validationMessages.trigger: Attachment file exceeds the 10MB limit (TEN_MB = 1024*1024*10)
validationMessages.evidence: ipaffs-file-upload-microservice/.../exceptions/RestResponseEntityExceptionHandler.java:17 (FILE_TOO_LARGE) + ValidationService.java:28,53-54 (MaxUploadSizeExceededException at TEN_MB); same constant MultiUploadValidationService.java:21. CORROBORATION: tests/document/document-upload-invalid-commercial-invoice.spec.ts:12 (upload large-file.pdf). Not observed in either captured trace.

validationMessages.message: Select a file
validationMessages.trigger: 'Next'/'Continue' pressed on the upload sub-step with no file chosen
validationMessages.evidence: ipaffs-file-upload-microservice/.../service/ValidationService.java:15,33-34 (FILE_NOT_SELECTED thrown when filename null/empty). Not observed in either captured trace.

validationMessages.message: The selected file contains a virus - upload failed
validationMessages.trigger: Anti-virus scan flags the uploaded attachment as infected
validationMessages.evidence: ipaffs-file-upload-microservice/.../resource/UploadResource.java:43 and antivirus/symantec/SymantecAntiVirusScanner.java:22. The frontend handler surfaces this via documentsUtil.handleInfectedFileValidation (routes/handlers/importer/documents.js:89). Answers part of the openQuestion about the intermediate virus-scan state between 'Next' and the documents list. Not observed in either captured trace.

validationMessages.message: The selected file could not be uploaded - Try again
validationMessages.trigger: Unexpected error during upload (e.g. storage failure)
validationMessages.evidence: ipaffs-file-upload-microservice/.../service/ValidationService.java:20-21 (UNEXPECTED_ERROR); cf. AttachmentStorageService.java:32 ('The selected file could not be uploaded - try again'). Not observed in either captured trace.

validationMessages.message: (row-conditional — see the individual legacy messages above)
validationMessages.trigger: Suspected mandatory-field errors on the documents row itself (missing document type / reference / issue date) — no test drives the empty-field path and no error trace captured one
validationMessages.evidence: No negative test and no rendered trace captured these. LEGACY NOW SUPPLIES THE COPY (see the document-type / document-reference / document-issue-date validationMessages entries and the per-field requiredEvidence): for CHED-D all three are required ONCE a row is started (documents_validation.js:44-108). What remains a genuine gap is only whether a rendered CHED-D trace matches this copy exactly — the 38 error-bearing traces should still be mined for a CHED-D documents-page error to upgrade legacy -> confirmed. The HTML `required` attribute was false in the trace because requiredness is enforced server-side by Joi, not via the native attribute.

openQuestions: LEGACY RESOLVED the mandatoriness/copy gap: for CHED-D (not isChedp) the document-type, document-reference and issue-date are all required ONCE a document row is started (documents_validation.js:44-108), with exact copy now captured in validationMessages (all `legacy`). Two things remain: (1) confirm against a rendered CHED-D error trace (mine the 38 error-bearing traces) to upgrade legacy -> confirmed; (2) note that requiredness is a FRONTEND/page policy — the schema AccompanyingDocument has NO CED-group @NotNull on these fields (AccompanyingDocument.java:32-40), so the rebuild may deliberately revisit whether a started-but-incomplete row must block Save, and whether at least one document is mandatory at all for CHED-D.

The document-type option VALUES are IPAFFS-internal codes (e.g. laboratorySamplingResultsForAflatoxin maps to visible 'Laboratory sampling results') and are data-driven from documentTypeMap(notification) (documents_validation.js:14,47) rather than a static list — the Joi .valid() allow-list is exactly that map's keys. The new app should confirm whether this list is fixed reference data or configurable per CHED type. The domain vocabulary types/document-type.ts holds 27 entries across all CHED types; only 14 render for CHED-D. Additionally the CHED-D-rendered label 'Laboratory sampling results' for value laboratorySamplingResultsForAflatoxin disagrees with the domain label 'Laboratory Sampling results for Aflatoxin (Reg 2019/1793)' (document-type.ts:15) — is the CHED-D dropdown re-labelling, or is the domain map stale? A human should reconcile.

The 'Add attachment' upload is a multi-step sub-flow (Select a file -> Next/Continue -> back to the documents list) with a server-side anti-virus scan in between. LEGACY filled more of this: the file-upload microservice enforces the allow-list, size bounds and virus scan (ValidationService.java; SymantecAntiVirusScanner 'The selected file contains a virus - upload failed'; frontend surfaces it via documentsUtil.handleInfectedFileValidation, documents.js:89). On success the attachment renders as a 'View <filename>' link (AccompanyingDocumentsPage.ts:31-33) and a 'Success' notification banner appears (documents.html:13-21). Still open: why the primary button label varies between 'Next' and 'Continue' (DocumentUploadPage.ts:14-16) — which appears when, and why?

DOCX QUESTION RESOLVED by legacy: the authoritative allow-list is {csv, doc, docx, jpg, jpeg, pdf, png, xlsx, xls, gif} (ValidationService.java:26-27). So DOCX, CSV, XLSX and GIF are all accepted despite the on-page hint (confirmed) advertising only 'DOC, JPEG, PDF, PNG or XLS' and the reject message naming a shorter set. A human should decide the authoritative allow-list AND the advertised copy for the new app so they agree (see discrepancies).

The minimum file size the message advertises ('larger than 1KB') does not match the enforced code threshold (200 bytes, TWO_HUNDRED_B — ValidationService.java:24-25,49-51). Which is correct for the rebuild?

The page is paginated (URL '/documents/page-1', hidden field current-page-number, 'Showing N additional documents') — pagination behaviour when many documents are added was not exercised by any trace or test. LEGACY: the paging partial is CHED-P only ({{#if isChedp}}{{> common/paging}}, documents.html:377-379), so CHED-D may not paginate the same way — confirm.
Page title differs between the initial list state ('Documents - ...') and the attachment sub-step ('Accompanying documents - ...'); inconsistency noted, not necessarily a requirement.

The saved document type/reference/attachment persist and are re-displayed downstream (inspector 'decision documents' view shows 'Health certificate' / 'REF-124' / 'example.png' — document-access.spec.ts:62-64), confirming this page is the capture point for that data. The inspector-side table (#inspector-documents-table, AccompanyingDocumentsPage.ts:35-37) is a separate view out of scope for the notifier journey.
discrepancies: File-type reject copy: LEGACY validator message is 'The selected file must be a DOC, JPEG, PDF, PNG, XLS or GIF' (ValidationService.java:16-17) and the real allow-list includes csv/docx/xlsx/gif (ValidationService.java:26-27), but the CONFIRMED on-page constraint hint reads 'file type must be a DOC, JPEG, PDF, PNG or XLS; ZIP files are not allowed' (trace b3742f19b6eea5d9285f5c8739571523a0a13ec1 action 42) — the rendered copy under-advertises the accepted types (no GIF/CSV/DOCX/XLSX). Rendered on-page copy is kept as confirmed; the divergence is a finding for Reconcile (align advertised copy with the enforced allow-list, or vice versa).

Minimum file size: the LEGACY message 'The selected file must be larger than 1KB' (ValidationService.java:18) and the CONFIRMED on-page hint 'larger than 1KB' both say 1KB, but the enforced code threshold is 200 bytes (TWO_HUNDRED_B; ValidationService.java:24-25,49-51). Message/hint vs code disagree — a finding for Reconcile.

Field-level requiredness source: the CONFIRMED traces show every documents-row input with required=false (native HTML attribute), yet LEGACY shows CHED-D enforces type/reference/date as required server-side via Joi once a row is started (documents_validation.js:44-108). Not a contradiction — requiredness is server-side, not native — but the rebuild should decide whether to surface it as a native `required`/aria-required for parity with GOV.UK error-handling guidance.
